channel_title: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
title: Claude Model Context Protocol (MCP): AI Has Tools Now and The Future Looks WILD
executive_summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a groundbreaking development that empowers AI models like Claude from Anthropic to use external tools, enhancing their functionality significantly. Currently, MCP requires manual JSON edits for integration, but it has already demonstrated its potential by enabling rapid workflows, such as creating a Google Maps lunch finder in just 90 seconds. By 2025, MCP could revolutionize AI usage, but only for those willing to explore its capabilities.
bullets: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) allows Claude to use tools like Google Maps, GitHub, and SQL databases, which is akin to how early humans used sticks and stones to build and create. This tool use unlocks a new level of AI power, enabling it to perform tasks beyond simple responses.

MCP requires users to edit JSON files to set up custom servers for Claude, which may feel cumbersome now. However, this process is already paving the way for powerful workflows, similar to how early computers needed complex coding to perform tasks that are now done with a click.

In its early stages, MCP has shown remarkable speed and simplicity, allowing users to create a Google Maps-based lunch finder in just 90 seconds. This rapid result is like assembling a sandwich in under a minute—quick, efficient, and satisfying.

MCP is compared to the introduction of HTTP in the early days of the web, which laid the groundwork for platforms like Google and Amazon. Just as HTTP revolutionized web interactions, MCP has the potential to transform how we engage with AI.

Currently, MCP requires manual configuration, but future versions are expected to simplify this process. Imagine a future where setting up AI tools is as easy as plugging in a toaster—just click and go.

By 2025, MCP could completely change how we use AI, but only for those who actively engage with its features. If you choose to ignore it, you might find yourself stuck with basic chatbot functionalities while others leverage advanced AI capabilities.

MCP transforms AI from a passive responder to an active collaborator, enhancing human creativity and problem-solving. This shift is like having a skilled partner in a brainstorming session, where ideas flow freely and solutions emerge collaboratively.

Nate B. Jones states, 'Tool use has come to AI. If you think this won’t revolutionize how we use AI, you’re missing the larger picture.' This emphasizes the significant impact that MCP will have on AI's role in our lives.

The flexibility of MCP allows for various integrations, which means developers can customize how Claude interacts with different tools. Think of it like a chef who can adjust recipes based on available ingredients, creating unique dishes each time.

MCP's current reliance on JSON editing may seem technical, but it opens doors to automated data retrieval and app prototyping. It's like learning to ride a bike—initially challenging, but once you master it, the freedom to explore is exhilarating.

MCP is still in its infancy, but its potential for workflow automation is already evident. This is similar to how smartphones evolved from basic mobile phones to powerful mini-computers, drastically changing how we communicate and work.

The future iterations of MCP are expected to offer native tools and click-to-configure options, making it accessible for casual users. Imagine a world where even someone with no technical background can set up their own AI tools with ease.
key_takeaways: Explore the Model Context Protocol (MCP) now to understand its capabilities, as waiting until 2025 could leave you behind in AI advancements. Engaging with MCP today will prepare you for the future of AI collaboration and innovation.

Set a goal to familiarize yourself with JSON editing for MCP integrations, as this skill will be crucial for leveraging AI tools effectively. Ignoring this could result in missed opportunities for automation and efficiency in your projects.

Encourage your team to experiment with MCP's capabilities, as this will foster creativity and innovation. If you don't actively explore these tools, you risk stagnating while others advance in their AI applications.

technical_terms.Model Context Protocol: A framework that allows AI models like Claude to interact with external tools and systems. Think of it like a universal remote that can control multiple devices in your home.
technical_terms.JSON: A lightweight data format used for storing and exchanging information between a server and a web application. Like a neatly organized filing cabinet, it helps keep data structured and easy to access.
technical_terms.Claude: An AI model developed by Anthropic that utilizes the Model Context Protocol to enhance its capabilities. Think of Claude as a smart assistant that can learn and adapt to various tasks.
technical_terms.HTTP: A protocol used for transferring hypertext requests and information on the internet. Like the postal service for the web, it ensures that data gets delivered to the right place.
technical_terms.Google Maps: A web mapping service that provides directions, location information, and navigation. Think of it like a digital compass that helps you find your way in unfamiliar territory.
technical_terms.GitHub: A platform for version control and collaboration on software development projects. Like a shared workspace for coders, it allows teams to work together efficiently on code.
technical_terms.SQL: A programming language used for managing and querying databases. Think of it like a librarian who helps you find specific books in a vast library.
technical_terms.Workflow Automation: The process of streamlining repetitive tasks through technology. Like an assembly line in a factory, it increases efficiency by reducing manual effort.
technical_terms.API Integration: The process of connecting different software applications to work together. Think of it like a bridge that allows two islands to connect and share resources.
technical_terms.Collaboration: Working together to achieve a common goal. Like a team of rowers in a boat, effective collaboration ensures everyone is moving in the same direction.
